sourcePolicy: REAL primary regulation and authoritative secondary sources only; no prior SUIT document used.

regionalContext.instrument: African Union Convention on Cyber Security and Personal Data Protection (Malabo Convention)
regionalContext.effect: Not directly binding on South African institutions; supplies continental policy baseline against which POPIA, the Cybercrimes Act and the ECT Act are read. No transposition/transfer obligation flows to ZA institutions.

notes: South Africa has no standalone unified Cybersecurity Act and no EU-style mutual-recognition or adequacy-list mechanism; cross-border transfer adequacy is self-assessed by the responsible party.

South Africa has no dedicated statutory web-accessibility standard; the digital-accessibility obligation is principle-based (PEPUDA reasonable accommodation + Constitution), with WCAG used as the de facto benchmark.
Functional obligations map to a single national jurisdiction (no member-state transposition layer); the Malabo Convention is regional context only, not an operative transfer mechanism for ZA.

functions.title: Security of processing of personal data
functions.instrument: Protection of Personal Information Act 4 of 2013 (POPIA), Condition 7, section 19
functions.deadlineOrMechanism: Continuous obligation: appropriate, reasonable technical and organisational measures on a risk-based standard to secure integrity and confidentiality of personal information against loss, damage, unauthorised destruction and unlawful access/processing.
functions.note: Direct national statutory duty; no threshold/size carve-out.

functions.title: Personal-data breach notification
functions.deadlineOrMechanism: Notify the Information Regulator and affected data subjects as soon as reasonably possible after discovery of the compromise (subject to legitimate law-enforcement / integrity-restoration needs). No fixed hour-count deadline.
functions.note: Notification content prescribed by s.22(5); Regulator may direct public notification.

functions.title: Cyber-incident / offence reporting (NIS-type)
functions.authority: South African Police Service (SAPS), with national 24/7 point of contact
functions.deadlineOrMechanism: Electronic communications service providers and financial institutions must report a qualifying offence to SAPS without undue delay and, where feasible, not later than 72 hours after becoming aware, in prescribed form, and preserve assisting information.
functions.note: No standalone unified Cybersecurity Act; Cybercrimes Act is the operative incident/offence instrument. Applies when the university acts as an electronic communications service provider.

functions.title: Cross-border / transborder data transfer
functions.instrument: POPIA section 72 (Transfers of personal information outside the Republic)
functions.authority: Information Regulator (South Africa) (oversight); responsible party self-assesses adequacy
functions.deadlineOrMechanism: Transfer permitted only where adequate protection (substantially similar to POPIA) is ensured by: foreign law / binding corporate rules / a binding agreement; OR data-subject consent; OR contractual necessity. No EU-style adequacy decision list; adequacy is self-assessed and documented per transfer.
functions.note: Transfer mechanism = binding-agreement / BCR / law adequacy / consent / contractual-necessity, self-certified by the responsible party.

functions.title: Electronic transactions, trust services and electronic signatures (eIDAS-type)
functions.instrument: Electronic Communications and Transactions Act 25 of 2002 (ECT Act), ss.13 and 37-40 (advanced electronic signature; accreditation)
functions.authority: South African Accreditation Authority (SAAA), under the Department of Communications and Digital Technologies
functions.deadlineOrMechanism: Legal recognition of data messages and electronic signatures; advanced electronic signature (AES) requires a digital certificate from an accredited authentication service provider (ss.37-40) and carries a rebuttable presumption of validity. Accreditation is the trust mechanism (no EU-style cross-border mutual recognition).
functions.note: ECT Act cyber-offence provisions (ss.85-88) repealed by the Cybercrimes Act 19 of 2020.

functions.title: Digital accessibility / non-discrimination
functions.instrument: Promotion of Equality and Prevention of Unfair Discrimination Act 4 of 2000 (PEPUDA / Equality Act), s.9 and disability provisions
functions.authority: Equality Courts; South African Human Rights Commission (SAHRC)
functions.deadlineOrMechanism: No fixed deadline; standing duty not to unfairly discriminate on the ground of disability, to remove obstacles and to take reasonable-accommodation steps. No dedicated web-accessibility statute; obligation is principle-based with WCAG as de facto benchmark.
functions.note: Derived from constitutional equality right; enforced via Equality Courts, monitored by SAHRC.
